title: Woodland Biofuels' Ambitious Carbon Negative RNG and Ultra-Green Hydrogen Production Facility in Louisiana
content: In an announcement that could prove to be significant for the United States RNG market, Canada's Woodland Biofuels has committed to make operational an RNG and ultra-green hydrogen plant valued at $ 1.35 billion in the state of Louisiana by the 2028. The project is touted to be the "world's largest carbon negative RNG" facility with carbon sequestering technologies in place. Being the leading RNG producing state, the investment could provide further impetus to the Louisianaâ€™s Climate Action Plan and to proximate clean fuels markets in the country. The project is expected to remove over 800,000 tonnes of CO2 annually, Phases 1 and 2 combined. The inevstment has received significant financial and infrastructure incentives from the state of Louisiana worth close to $250 million.
author: Analysts Account
